title: Assata Shakur: clips from her 1980 address to the people after her escape from prison
description: This program is an edited version of her address to the people after she escaped. She begins the recording by stating that she loves everyone and encourages everyone to continue to struggle for our liberation. Assata talks about how Black people are constantly under attack by the poor school system, infiltration of drugs, welfare system, police state, etc. She talks about the necessity for a Black Nation and how Black women must play a key role in the struggle for liberation. She says that they are the most closely related to the struggle because of their position in White American society. As

title: Assata Shakur on African Women [mp3]
description: Assata Shakur on the importance of Black women participating and leading the Black Liberation Struggle.

title: Assata Shakur on her core values [mp3]
description: Assata Shakur speaks on her core values (freedom and justice for all people) and the importance of African women participating in every aspect of the struggle.

title: Assata Shakur: Carry It On (The Tradition) [mp3]
description: The Tradition written by Assata Shakur.

title: Barbara Lubinski Reads What is Left [mp3]
description: Barbara Lubinki reads What is Left by Assata Shakur.

title: Words from a Sister in Exile
description: When revolutionary political activist Assata Shakur (previously JoAnne Chesimard) made a daring escape from prison in 1979, she- like our fugative slave ancestors- became legendary in the Black community. Here she speaks about her life in Cuba today.

title: Geronimo Ji Jaga from COINTELPRO 101 (Video Clip #1)
description: High-ranking member of the Black Panther Party and human rights activist. Wrongly convicted, through a COINTELPRO operation, of murder and spent 27 years in prison.

title: Geronimo Ji Jaga from COINTELPRO 101 [Video Clip]
description: High-ranking member of the Black Panther Party and human rights activist. Wrongly convicted, through a COINTELPRO operation, of murder and spent 27 years in prison.

title: Geronimo Pratt: National Black Human Rights Coalition (NBHRC) - Video Clip
description: Lift Every Voice and Sing/Intro -> Illian (entertainment) -> Victor of National Council of Black Lawyers (NCBL) -> Lewis Myers (lawyer for Geronimo Pratt) -> Horace Tapscott (jazz musician) -> Mamado -> Muntu Matsimela (chairman National Black Human Rights Coalition) -> Watani -> Ashaki Pratt (Geronimo's wife) -> Louis Farrakhan -> End.

title: Geronimo Ji Jaga on Black Liberation
description: Geronimo Ji Jaga explains the emergence of the Black Panther party as a small piece of the Black Liberation movement.

title: Growing up in the Black Nation
description: Geronimo Ji Jaga reflects on how growing up in the Black Nation among enemy forces gave him a deep respect for defenders of the community.

title: Excerpt from SLA Support Rally
description: Excerpt taken from KPFA Memorial Show for the Symbionese Liberation Army (SLA) 6/17/1974.

title: For the Symbionese Liberation Army
description: A poem written by "a sister in the Weather Underground" to the Symbionese Liberation Army. The poem warns of who the real terrorists are and supports revolutionary struggle.

title: Huey Says Panthers are Not Parties to Extortion
description: A letter from Huey P. Newton of the Black Panther Party to Mr. and Mrs. Randolph Hearst. The letter volunteers the Black Panthers to distribute the food as part of the bargain from the SLA to return Patricia Hearst to her family.

title: Revolution? -- Or Education Through the Media
description: This article comments on the kidnapping of Patricia Hearst and the role of mass media in meeting the left's demands.

title: Weather Underground February 20, 1974
description: This pamphlet is from the Weather Underground and commends the SLA for kidnapping Patricia Hearst in order to give food to the poor people. Bernadine Dohrn offers four points in response to the war between the rich and the poor.

title: Why Assata Shakur Must Be Supported
description: This statement includes analysis of Assata Shakur's case in the context of rising support among feminist groups. The statement asserts the primary importance of her case in relationship to the Black Liberation Movement.

title: They're Talking at Sing Sing
description: Photo with BLA sign from cell

title: Angela Davis Release Speech [mp3]
description: Angela Davis, African-American leader, scholar, and prison abolitionist. She was charged in connection with events at the Marin Courthouse on August 7, 1970. this is from her first statement after her acquittal.

title: Conspiracy Against Angela Exposed
description: Flyer issued by the L.A. Committee to Free Angela Davis, which comments on the excessive expense of time and government money in the pursuit of putting together a case to convict Angela Davis of murder, kidnapping, and conspiracy. Outlines the prosecutor's failure to prove his case.

title: On Trial: Angela Davis or America?
description: Booklet surrounding Angela Davis's imprisonment and trial, where she was convicted of murder, kidnapping, and conspiracy. Includes an introduction by Ossie Davis, excerpts from two speeches--"I Bring An Indictment Against the American System" by Reverend Ralph Abernathy and "The Case Must be Dismissed" by Attorney John J. Abt--and an article, "In Defense of the Rights of Angela Davis" by Paul E. Miller.

title: Lectures on Liberation
description: Collection of transcripts from Angela Davis's initial lectures for "Recurring Philosophical Themes in Black Literature," her first course at UCLA taught during the Fall Quarter of 1969. Lectures outline the causes and perpetuation of oppression. They also deal with the psychological nature of the oppressor and the oppressed, the process by which the latter becomes conscious of their own oppression and the way in which they triumph over it. Includes introduction describing Davis's appointment as Assistant Professor at UCLA, the setting and size of her classes, and the Regents of the University

title: Remember Attica: Reconstruction, Reflection, Reaction [mp3]
description:
Part V of the “Remember Attica” series. Responses of clergy, attorneys, doctors, congressmen and the prisoner’s relatives in the weeks following the rebellion. Lawyers were admitted on 9/17/71. A week later the National Lawyers Guild gave a press conference outside the prison. They concluded that Rockefeller, and prison officials (Oswald, Mancusi and Vincent) provoked the attack by giving wrongful information to guards and troopers.
On 9/17/71 BUILD, black community activists in Buffalo, went to Meyer Memorial Hospital where seriously wounded prisoners had been taken. BUILD’s executive directo

title: Introductory Remarks from CNB [mp3]
description: Introductory remarks re: political/historical context for the Attica Rebellion.

title: Attica Prison Rebellion
description: Frank "Big Black" Smith and L.D. Barkley, who proclaimed the Attica Manifesto, and Elizabeth Fink, an attorney for the Attica Brothers. Barkley was killed in the massacre. Smith survived mass torture to help lead the eventually successful legal battle on behalf of the Attica Brothers.

title: Attica is All of Us
description: September 9-13 mark the 44th anniversary of the Attica Rebellion. This massive prison takeover by hundreds of inmates and the callous repression and murders by the state of New York are part of a unique moment in US history. The legacy of Attica and the fight for human rights is carried on in the prisons of Georgia, Ohio, California and wherever people are caged for years on end.

title: Attica: Then and Now
description: Monograph focusing on the Attica Prison Rebellion, its causes, the aftermath and the campaign to free Dacajeweiah. Contents include: Introduction; Prisons: Who Goes and Why?; The Movement Grows; The Uprising; The Prosecution; Who is Dacajeweiah; What is Being Done; Epilogue.

title: Fred Hampton Speaks [mp3]
description: Short clip of Illinois Black Panther Party Chairman Fred Hampton speaking at the University of Chicago.

title: Remember Fred Hampton Mark Clark
Afeni Shakur: Lessons from Panther 21 Trail
description: Afeni Shakur draws lessons from the Panther 21 trail and encourages people to support political prisoners by attending court sessions as witnesses.

title: Afeni Shakur: Racial Solidarity in Prisons
description: Afeni Shakur speaks about the importance of being in solidarity with political prisoners, Sam Melville at Attica prison, how Attica uprising is a prime example on how racial solidarity can be effective and how even prison guards can be supportive of incarcerated revolutionaries.

title: Afeni Shakur: Solidarity during Panther 21 Trail
description: Afeni Shakur speaks about the constant support the 21 Panthers received from people who showed up every day to the trails, including school children, and how this has affected the jury positively. In addition, she addresses the complex relation between political prisoners and guards and how some guards showed gestures of solidarity by, for instance, fasting with political prisoners.

title: Afeni Shakur: Joining Black Panther Party
description: Afeni Shakur recollects the first time meeting Bobby Seal in New York City who was speaking out against the police and acknowledges that what attracted her to the Black Panther Party in the beginning was romanization of liberation struggle.

title: Richard Dhoruba (bin Wahad) Moore - Tape 1 of 2
description: Part one (part two - PM 145) of a two-part interview with Richard Dhoruba (Bin Wahad) Moore from prison at the House of Detention in New York. Moore speaks in defense of the Black Liberation Army and the revolutionaries who have been imprisoned or killed in the struggle. The role of the New York police department is highlighted in the killing of Frank Fields, Anthony White and others. The media portrayal of Sam Napier’s death as the result of a feud between rival Panther factions is examined. Without validating this claim Moore discusses his perception of the strengths and weaknesses of the mo

title: Richard Dhoruba (bin Wahad) Moore Interview - Part 2 of 2
description: Part Two (Part One - PM 143) of a two-part interview with Richard Dhoruba (Bin Wahad) Moore from the House of Detentions prison in New York. Moore advocates the “tactical necessity” of creating mass movements to support political prisoners. Frustration is voiced over the choice of certain parts of the progressive movement that have distanced themselves from those who advocate revolutionary armed struggle and the consequences of this alienation. Moore discusses the case of Ruchell Magee, and the contradiction faced by a revolutionary movement working within the legal system. He also details his

title: An Unpublished letter
description: Unpublished letter to the editor of the New York Times in which Dhoruba critiques an inaccurate editorial published earlier in the NYTimes.

title: Interview with Kiilu Nyasha
description: Kiilu Nyasha interviews Dhoruba Bin Wahad shortly after he was released from 19 years in prison as a political prisoner for a crime he did not commit. They speak about a number of topics including Geronimo Ji-Jaga, the prison movement and political prisoners still in captivity.

title: Notes on the Black Panther Party - It's Basic Working Papers and Policy Statements
description: This pamphlet is an attempt to provide you with the basic working documents of the Black Panther Party. These basic documents are the foundation from which all the theory and practice that flowed from the Black Panther Party outward was produced. It is impossible to receive a clear and undistorted picture of what the Black Panther Party was truly about without an understanding of the politics that guided it...This pamphlet, though not all inclusive, will lay open under one cover, the basic tenets of the Black Panther Party and ask you to read them and try and understand them. - Safiya Bukhari-

title: Lest We Forget
description: A booklet commemorating Black Panther Party members who were assassinated, containing an introduction by Safiya A. Bukhari, poetry, illustrations, and biographical information about those who were murdered. Photocopied.

title: Still Angry - After All These Years
description: Reproduction of transcripted interview with Donald Cox, former Field Marshal of the Black Panther Party.

title: Application for Repatriation and Dual Citizenship for African American Political Prisoners of War and Ex-Political Prisoners
description: Historical Persepctive on Political Prisoners and POWs from the Black Liberation Movement being held in the US. Includes profiles on political prisoners and the Geneva Convention Regulations as related to prisoners of war.

title: New African Ujamaa: The Economics of the Republic of New Africa
description: Declaration of Economic Intent: Basic Principles, Prestige and Leisure Activity, Imperatives for Success, Start Money for a New Community, The Needs, The Sources, Making the Economy Run.

title: The New Afrikan: The Official Organ of the Republic of New Afrika [November 1979]
description: Cover Story: RNA Presidential Tour Major Success; Two RNA-11 Members Released, Imari Denied Parole. Also contains articles on: Boston's first New Afrikan Family Conference; Imari Obadele Denied Parole. A Tribute to Revolutionary Black Womanhood; RNA Presidential Tour; The Issue is Really Which Way to Sovereignty; Towards Building Black Human Rights; Prosecution of Pontiac Brothers Continues; more.

title: The New Afrikan: The Official Organ of the Republic of New Afrika - V 9-3 - Dec 18, 1983
description: Black Nation Day Brings Unity To RNA. Also, New Afrika Wins Brinks Case, Fulani is Free, Inside the Brinks Story (with specific focus on the political prisoners captured as a result of that action), Dark Days in Grenada (on the US invasion of Grenada), New Afrikan Political Scene, more.

title: The Story of Dessie Woods
description: "A Close-Up Look at Justice in the 'New South.'" Dessie Woods was imprisoned after being charged, tried and convicted for defending herself from a rape attempt by a white man on June 17th, 1975.

title: Justice amerikkkan style in the DESSIE WOODS CASE
description: Defense lawyers on behalf of Dessie Woods have filed a writ of habeas corpus with the middle District Federal Court in Macon, GA. The writ of habeas corpus documents that many state and federal violations of Dessie Woods' rights, and asks the court to show cause as to why in light of these violations, Dessie Woods is still being held in prison. The writ is reprinted here in its entirety.

title: Night of Solidarity
description: Statement from the Dessie Woods Support Coalition.

title: Self Respect, Self Defense & Self Determination - Full program
description: An event held at the First Congregational Church in Oakland on Sunday, March 14, 2004 with Mabel Williams and Kathleen Cleaver. Both women were welcomed and introduced by Angela Davis. These two inspiring women of the 60s Black Liberation struggle met to share their personal experiences - resisting the KKK and police repression, forced into exile government repression, and their international experiences in Third World nations. Mabel Williams, with her late husband Robert F. Williams, met with Malcolm X, Ho Chi Minh, Che Guevara and Mao Tse Tung to help internationalize support for the Black L

title: Mabel Williams on the Beginnings of Radio Free Dixie
description: Mabel Williams recounts the story behind the beginnings of Radio Free Dixie when her and her husband Robert were living in Cuba.

title: Mabel Williams on Armed Self-Defense and the Klan
description: Mabel Williams speaks on the racist and dangerous conditions in Monroe, North Carolina that necessitated armed self-defense for the Black Community. She also talks about how her husband (Robert F. Williams) on one hand knew that the law was not made to protect Black people but on the other hand used it to his advantage when it came to the right to bear arms.

title: Mabel Williams Recounts the Story of Her Family's Flight from Monroe to Cuba.
description: Mabel Williams recounts the story of how her family was forced to flee their home in Monroe, North Carolina. She specifically mentions the importance of the networks that they ha previously established and how those friends were vital in sustaining the family during their flight and eventually getting them to Cuba.

title: The Life and Times of Richard Aoki: in his own words
description: ￼The Life and Times of Richard Aoki in his own words. From Richard Aoki's interview with KPFA Apex Reporter, Wayie Ly Taped July 2006

title: Richard Aoki Interviews with KPFA Reporter Wayie
description: Interview with Richard Aoki from July 2006. 16 questions; 22 page transcript.

title: New Afrikan Freedom Fighters - V 1-1 - June 1982
description: Statement of Purpose; Principles of Unity; FBI Terrorize Black Health Center; New Afrikan Prisoners of War: United States Has No Jurisdiction; Sundiata Acoli in daring Escape Attempt; The Necessity of Armed Freedom Fighters; The Saga of Twyman Myers; New Afrikan Freedom Fighters Day; Speech by Julio Rosado Movimiento de Liberacion Nacional [MLN]; War, Undeclared or not, is still WAR!; New Afrikan Freedom Fighters Day; FALN Communique.

title: New Afrikan Freedom Fighter - V 2-1 - March 1983
description: Periodical on Black Liberation; containing numerous articles highlighting the right and duty for Black and African peoples' to question and resist the violence of the U.S. government by any means. Also includes messages of solidarity for political prisoners; statements in direct opposition to state collaborators/ infiltrators; as well as articles that advocate for solidarity with other international movements against colonialism and U.S. imperialism. Cover stories include: Enemies of the People! Death to all Traitors!; "Vigilance Against Traitors and Treason"; "On the Armed Front"; "A Chronolo

title: Interview with Josefina Rodriguez
description: 15 minute Interview with Josefina Rodriguez as part of a longer KPFA radio show. Rodriguez talks about her role as the International Representative for the Movimiento de Liberacion Nacional, her daughters (political prisoners) Alicia and Ida Luz Rodriguez, their political development, the struggle for independence, the charge of seditious conspiracy, the Lexington Pennintentiary control unit, etc.. At end, Side B cuts to music from side A.

title: Puerto Rican nueva trova (?) music
description: Puerto Rican music program on KPFA. 1970s/80s Nueva trova-ish. Fuzzy sound. Also on very end of LA 071 B.

title: Lolita Lebron on Puerto Rican Independence [mp3]
description: A clip taken from a conversation between Puerto Rican independista Lolita Lebron and interviewer Barbara Lubinski. At the time, Lolita was being held in Alderson Federal Prison for her role in the 1954 Attack on Congress. Taken from CD 884.

title: Alejandrina Torres on Puerto Rican Independence [mp3]
description: Alejandrina Torres speaks about Puerto Rican independence and US repression of their national liberation struggle. Taken from CD 800.

title: Puerto Rican Sovereignty and National Defense [mp3]
description: Jose Lopez of the New Movement Conference speaks about the movement in Puerto Rico within the world context. This clip focuses specifically on the prospect of Puerto Rican sovereignty and the need for national defense capabilities. Lopez asserts that the United States wants to see Puerto Rico tied to them economically as to disallow them complete independence, while not allowing them statehood rights either.

title: Death to the Klan! Summer 1986
description: In this Issue: Klan Using Cable TV to Reach Millions; Centers for Black Survival; A look at the Puerto Rican Independence Movement; Fighting for Freedom (News on Political Prisoners); Commentary on Libya. Also, Chicago to be "racist capitol of the word" says Klan; Marines Train NC Klan; Mobilizations Against Prison Control Units; Sandinistas Fight Amerikkka's Contras; Carrying on the Tradition of John Brown (by Marilyn Buck); Who is Mutulu Shakur; Stop Big Mountain Relocation; Azania: the Land Belongs to the People.

title: Nazi Skinheads Active in San Francisco
description: The John Brown Anti-Klan Committee, joined by anti-racist punks and community activists is beginning a campaign to alert the community to this growing cancer in our midst and help organize people to stop Nazi terrorists. This campaign features media and educational work to expose the links to organized white supremacist violence while support community action in opposition to racism.

title: No KKK! No Fascist USA! - Spring/Summer 1989
description: Newspaper of the John Brown Anti-Klan Committee. In this issue: unity between the Ku Klux Klan and Neo-Nazis; Tom Metzger; the formation of the "Syndicate", an anti-racist organization made up of anti-racist skinheads; the origins of fascist and Neo-Nazi movements in Europe; an overview of Nazi white-supremacist actions throughout the U.S.; anti-racist response to Nazi Skinheads; the murder of an Ethiopian man by Nazi skinheads; KKK run out of Atlanta; Anti-Racist Resource List; Imari Obadele on the implications of the term "African-American"; book review of Fire on the Mountain; update on the

title: Death to the Klan! November 1979
description: This is the first issue of the newsletter of the John Brown Anti-Klan Committee. Articles include Assata Shakur's escape from prison, an overview of advances in the Black liberation movement, an update on the fight to free the Pontiac Brothers, the murder by white police of a Black man in Texas, an editorial about Klan terror, the fight against white supremacy at City University of New York (CUNY), and information about a forum addressing the struggle to free the land for the Republic of New Afrika. This issue also includes the "Principles of Unity" outlining what white people need to do to ac

title: Death to the Klan! December 1979
description: This issue of the newsletter includes articles about Klan activity, Zionist and white supremacist unity in opposition to Arab and Black liberation, the struggle against gentrification in Brooklyn, a march against the Klan in Texas, the assassination of Puerto Rican freedom fighter Angel Rodriguez Cristobal, a forum on U.S. imperialism and the struggle to free the Black nation, brutality against women in New Hampshire. The issue also includes the "Principles of Unity" which outlines what white people need to do to actively oppose white supremacy.

title: Breakthrough (Winter-Spring 1987)
description: Editorials: Puerto Rico: Yankee Go Home p. 1 - Defend the Palestinian Revolution p. 4 - Contragate p. 6 - Chicago: Confronting the Racist Right p. 7 - Myth of Black Progress, New Afrikan People's Organization p. 17 - Kuwasi Balagoon 1946-1986 p. 21 - U.S. and Zaire Training African Contras p. 22 - Chile: Crisis in the Southern Cone p. 24 - Interview with Puerto Rican Patriot Rafael Cancel Miranda p. 31 - From the Clandestine Movement: Communiques from the Puerto Rican People's Army - Machateros p. 38 - On the Chicago Indictments p. 40 - Shut Down the Lexington Control Unit p. 41 - Statement by

title: Breakthrough (March 1977)
description: Breakthrough - an Introduction p. 2 - Provisional Statement of PFOC p. 6 section on women's oppression & liberation p. 28 - Meaning of Chicago (class and revolutionary politics) p. 57 - Open Letter to the RC from Native American Warriors p. 59 - To My People by Assata Shakur p. 61

title: Breakthrough (June-July 1977)
description: Introducing Breakthrough No. 2 p. 1 - On the Conviction of Leonard Peltier with a reprint of a support statement by the Native Study Group of Vancouver, British Columbia p. 4 - The Guardian Sets Out to Build Itself a Party, a PFOC critique p. 9 - The Guardian on Women's "Emancipation" - a proposal to move backwards by Flynn and friends - Burning Spear: reprints from the African People's Socialist Party on Bourgeois Ideology on the Left and on Women in Struggle p. 22 - Assata Convicted with a message from Assata p. 31 - Lenin on Guerrilla Warfare p. 34 - a Comment on Puerto Rico Solidarity Work

title: Breakthrough (Spring 1978)
description: Anti-imperialist struggle sharpening; PFOC self-criticism p. 1 - Palestine will win! P. 8 -I declare (poem by Mahmoud Darweesh) p. 23 - IWY Houston new stage of struggle p. 24 - notes from an Afrikan POW journal by New Afrikan Prisoners Organization p. 37 - Press statement by the African People's Socialist Party p. 52 - Free the L.A. Five p. 54 - speech by Angel Rodriquez Cristobal of Liga Socialist Puertorriquena, Chicago, 1978 p. 65 - Armed Struggle and Revolution p. 71 - Gotta be Strong (poem) p. 84 - Join in Solidarity with the German R.A.F. p. 86

title: Arm the Spirit: For Revolutionary Resistance
Arm the Spirit: For Revolutionary Resistance
Arm the Spirit: For Revolutionary Resistance
Arm The Spirit - First Edition
description: First Issue - formerly Voices From Within (the one and only issue was banned hence the name change) Cover Story: Message from the Editors; The Current Situation in the Political Prisoner Unit AKA Management Control Unit at California State Prison at San Quentin; Lewisburg political prisoners made scapegoat for prison fires (Michael Ashanti Alston); A draft proposal for a national Afrikan prisoner organization (Lorenzo Komboa Ervin); Letter from Yaki; US prisoners petition to the United Nations; California to build more prisons; Herman Bell speaks: the story of an African Prisoner of War

title: Arm the Spirit No. 4
description: Articles include: Honor Fallen Freedom Fighters: Build Black August Month, Message from S.Q. Adjustment Center, Black Cultural Development and Revolutionary Growth, Pontiac Update, Report from Folsom: Did Folsom Prison Officials have Complicity in Racist Attack on Blacks, Lewisburg Manifesto, B.L.A Under Attack, Call to Build a National Black Human Rights Coalition, Black Prison Guards, A Brief History on the Formation of ZANLA, Close Marion Control Unit!

title: Arm the Spirit No. 14
description: Articles include: Support New African Freedom Fighters; The Necessity of Armed Fighters; From Sekou Odinga- New Afrikan Prisoner of War; New Afrikan Freedom Fighters Day; National Conference Strengths Struggle Against Repression (Jose Lopez); Grand Jury Resistors Arrested; FALN Bombs Wall Street; Statements from Political Prisoners; Zionism is Racism: Palestine Will Win; Bloody Riots at Pontiac; FBI Frame-Up of Peltier Exposed; DQ Under Attack; Anti-Springbok 5 Sentenced; more.

title: Harriet Tubman
description: greeting card

title: Counterinsurgency in the Courtroom: The "Resistance Conspiracy Case"
description: Reproduction of an article about the case by "Resistance Conspiracy Case" defendant Laura Whitehorn.

title: International Day to Stop Violence Against Women Solidarity Statement
description: Statement of solidarity for the International Day to Stop Violence Against Women from the women of the "Resistance Conspiracy Case"
